Name: The Bridge Inside
Description: The Bridge Inside is a novel about psychedelic therapy. It's a narrative fiction that follows the story of a woman affected with PTSD who undergoes psychedelic therapy. A dreamscape which traces the main characters dreams as they turn to nightmares and then therapy sessions, The Bridge Inside paints a picture of psychedelic therapy as a means of exploring our own psyche, similar to dreams, that can lead to self-healing. Based on real life practices, the novel explores modern therapeutic practices which have been most successful for PTSD and illustrates its effectiveness in a palatable way. The road to healing is paved within us all, once we choose to cross that bridge.

Welcome to the World of Jaybie D. - Look beyond what you can see. Unleash your mind and set it free!

 Books by Jaybie D.:

Donkey Dell (Ages 5-8): Dell channels his determined strengths in an unexpected and inspiring way to save the circus.  It's a demonstration of bold commitment and courage in the face of adversity.

Mei-Mei Flying With Flair (Ages 5-8): Meet Mei-Mei, a curious fairy who discovers the power of resilience, self-reflection and diverse perspectives after an unexpected collision with a flock of birds. 

Haunted House Day & Night (Ages 5-8): Confront your deepest fears during a family trip to a haunted house - not just once, but twice! 

Fangs, Fairies & Follies (Ages 3-5): Join Silas the vampire on his quest to fix his flat fangs with the help of the Tooth Fairy.

 Coming Soon Books: 

Whisker Watchers (Ages 3-5; August 15): A secret society of feline purr-tectors chasing away candy loving monsters. A fun and thrilling introduction to the excitement of Halloween. 

Rascally Reflection (Ages 5-8; Date TBD): A rascally mirror helps Vicky to see herself and others in an empathetic way. A journey of self confidence and acceptance with heart.

Beware The Guarded Heart (Ages 5-8; Date TBD): Aidan struggles to share and build relationships. He learns there is value in creating space for others after being whisked away to a magical land.

 Accolades and Recognition:
Proud recipient of the Mom's Choice Awards, Creative Child Magazine's Book of the Year Award, and numerous other accolades (details on the About page at www.JaybieD.com). 

 Educational Resources:
I offer free teacher lesson plans, book trailers and more, making my books a valuable resource for educators.

Description:
The Phoenix Archetype: Reclaim Your Power Through Strength and Strategy

Uncover the proven framework to conquer life’s toughest challenges and rebuild with clarity, confidence, and purpose.

When betrayal, loss, or uncertainty shatter your foundation, your path forward isn’t about starting over—it’s about unlocking the strengths and strategies already within you. The Phoenix Archetype offers a battle-tested model built on decades of respected research in human performance psychology and crisis management, tailored to the moments when life tests you the most.

Drawing on her certification in training and development for Fortune 100 teams, extensive consulting experience, and personal journey of overcoming intense opposition, Kristy Dunn provides powerful tools to help you:

• Discover your personal archetype to thrive under pressure.
• Unlock your hidden keys to breakthrough and navigate life’s challenges with confidence.
• Turn obstacles into opportunities by leveraging strengths and mitigating blind spots.
• Rebuild with purpose and resilience, both personally and professionally.

If life has thrown you into chaos, The Phoenix Archetype is your key to clarity and thriving again.

Your breakthrough begins today. Let The Phoenix Archetype guide you to rise stronger. Visit phoenixarchetype.com to learn more.

Name: CCI Black Book
Description: The CCI Black Book is the most comprehensive and modern approach to cannabis cultivation. We built a scientific approach to increase yield and maximize efficiencies. Embracing new technology, digital tracking tools, and automated systems, creating a proven process. Eliminating guesswork using tested, established tracking, numbers, and environmental setpoints, to keep your rooms growing at their maximum potential. The book begins with explaining the scientific ideology behind what drove the approach. Then moving into to our step-by-step process, taking grow cycles from start to finish. Lessons cover each phase of the plant’s life: mothers, clones, vegetative, flowering, drying, trimming, and curing. Along with pest research, room maintenance, and technology instruction. The CCI Black Book teaches the basics of cultivation to the more in-depth nuances of crop steering. It was created for cultivators of all levels of expertise, from home growers to commercial growers alike.

Name: Tills Bookshop
Description:
Established in 1985 by Rick and Ann Till, Tills Bookshop is one of Edinburgh's oldest surviving second-hand bookshops. Overlooking the Meadows park, our wee shop has carved out a small literary corner in Edinburgh's South Side. When Rick and Ann retired, the shop was taken over and renovated in 2019 by Kate and Joshua McNamara.

The shop is very small, but we've crammed it with a wide ranging library of beautiful books to serve the voracious readers of Edinburgh.

Open seven days a week, with a big selection of literary fiction, both contemporary and classic, quirky sci-fi, history and biography, philosophy and sociology, and the odd antiquarian gem. There are a few seats, and staff ready for all manner of bookish chats.

Name: Feria de Editoriales Independientes de Venezuela (FEIV)
Underdog Bookstore
Description:
We are readers who cherish books that reflect our own stories. We want to build a community of underdogs—those who spent their childhoods looking for characters that looked like them. The team at Underdog Bookstore is diverse—indigenous, latine, asian, queer, disabled, and author-owned—and we want our bookstore to reflect the whole community of Monrovia. 

For us, bookstores are more than consumerist hotspots. They are the places where you bond with your partner over a book that celebrates gay marriage, where you’ve bought your favorite candle from a local artisan, or where you see your work in print for the first time through a local writing competition.

Bookstores are a place where communities are celebrated and true underdogs—those who feel disadvantaged in society—can heal both by themselves and with others. Underdog Bookstore will give walls to the community of book lovers we’ve already so proudly started to build.

What makes us different? We don't just want to be a place people can buy books: we want to be an integral part of the community. We envision being part of people's love stories, helping them to find their forever friend at a pet adoption, and helping kids find the book that will engage them to be a life-long learner. Everything we do is motivated by community over profit, and books are the perfect centerpiece. Why? Because they change lives.

Books help people discover more about who they are, their race, their gender, their age, and so much more. Authors take on the precious task of sharing themselves with an audience. Sure, some of them make big money. But many of them, especially authors published by small and medium presses, do it for the love of the craft. We also love their craft and are eager to give them a platform. We are committed to diverse authors, but especially local authors. They record the history of our community.

Name: BookBite.io
Description:
We believe knowledge should be easy to absorb even on the go. BookBite is a digital-native startup focused on leveraging cutting-edge technology and AI to transform how people learn from books.

Founded in 2024, BookBite was born from a simple observation: incredible ideas are trapped in books that most people never read due to time constraints. We're changing that by combining expert curation, AI technology, and beautiful design to deliver knowledge in the format that fits modern life.

Our team of technologists, designers, and educators works tirelessly to curate, summarize, and produce high-quality audio content that captures the essence of each book. We're not just summarizing we're distilling wisdom and making it actionable.

BookBite was born from a simple observation: people want to learn and grow, but finding the time to read entire books is increasingly difficult in our fast-paced world.

We believe that knowledge shouldn't be locked behind hundreds of pages. The core insights from most non-fiction books can be effectively communicated in 10-15 minutes, allowing busy professionals, parents, and students to continue their learning journey without sacrificing precious time.

Our team of expert curators carefully reads and distills each book, preserving the author's key messages and most valuable insights. Professional narrators then bring these summaries to life, creating an engaging audio experience that you can enjoy anywhere, anytime.

Today, BookBite serves thousands of learners worldwide, helping them stay informed, inspired, and intellectually curious one bite-sized summary at a time.

Name: Leading with Care in a Tough World
Description:
Bob DeKoch and Phil Clampitt, long-time collaborators, draw on their years of innovative practice and research to describe with vivid, real-world experiences, how leading with care enriches people and transforms organizations in our challenging times. 

Gallup recently reported that only 15% of employees in the United States are truly engaged in their workplace. Yet nearly 70% of employees say they would work harder if they felt more appreciated. Over 90% of employees surveyed believed their leadership “lacked communication skills to lead.” The gloomy stories behind sobering statistics reveal employees’ disappointment with current leadership approaches. Leaders can do better. 

That’s why the authors sought to go beyond the laudable sentiments of the “servant leadership” movement by envisioning, creating, and testing a new leadership platform based on a set of guiding beliefs and actual day-to-day practices.

The authors’ unique perspective transforms traditional practices, like employee coaching and collaboration, into dynamic self-actualizing workplaces. How? By sharing how caring leaders adroitly blend visible and subtle practices to realize the aspirational benefits of coaching and collaboration. The authors use a similar approach to share insights about less frequently discussed leadership issues such as how to build an empowering network of relationships and transform pushback into progress. Anyone who wishes to craft their own distinctive, compelling, and empowering leadership style that unleashes the latent energy of people and their organizations will discover a robust and enriching framework for doing so in Leading with Care in a Tough World.

Създаване на инфопродукти: Разработка на различни информационни материали, включително менторски програми и уебинари.

Консултации: Безплатни консултации с Даяна Митева за стартиране на бизнес с книги и обучения. 

През 2023 г. агенцията е реализирала над 200 книги и десетки обучения, като 98% от клиентите са доволни от предоставените услуги.

Nosso manifesto é de contribuir para a formação de uma identidade cultural forte e coesa na cidade, estimulando os sentimentos de pertencimento e orgulho na população, fortalecendo os laços entre a comunidade.

O CAFÉ MAL ASSOMBRADO POA é uma iniciativa cultural da banda LÍTERA e da POA MAL ASSOMBRADA.

Name: Richard Booth's Bookshop
Description:
Built in 1886, by Robert Williams and Sons Ltd, 44 Lion Street was the largest commercial building and first Limited Company in the market town of Hay on Wye.

Carrying a full range of agricultural necessities, no trip to town on market day was complete without a visit to 44 Lion Street to stock up on seeds and plows, shears and fencing. There was even an on-site forge for metal repairs.

Later, having been acquired by Peter Likes, the business expanded to include furniture and homewares.

In 1982, Richard Booth was looking to consolidate his secondhand book empire in Hay, having run out of available smaller storefronts to house his burgeoning inventory.

“Miles” of bookshelves were built and a legend was born. Richard proclaimed (he liked a proclamation) that his bookshop was the largest in the world which might even have been true.

Richard sold his business to Paul Greatbatch and Elizabeth Haycox in 2007.

Richard Booth’s Bookshop honours its past by the careful conservation of this glorious building while we look to the future of bookselling which now includes secondhand and new books sharing shelfspace, a curated collection of craft materials and art supplies, greeting cards and gifts.

Just around the corner, we have turned an old warehouse space (where all those bookshelves were built) into a small independent cinema showing the best of current and classic films.

It is still true today that no trip to Hay on market day is complete without a visit to 44 Lion Street.

Name: Impact Networks
Description:
This practical guide shows how to facilitate collaboration among diverse individuals and organizations to navigate complexity and create change in our interconnected world.

The social and environmental challenges we face today are not only complex, they are also systemic and structural and have no obvious solutions. They require diverse combinations of people, organizations, and sectors to coordinate actions and work together even when the way forward is unclear. Even so, collaborative efforts often fail because they attempt to navigate complexity with traditional strategic plans, created by hierarchies that ignore the way people naturally connect.
 
By embracing a living-systems approach to organizing, impact networks bring people together to build relationships across boundaries; leverage the existing work, skills, and motivations of the group; and make progress amid unpredictable and ever-changing conditions. As a powerful and flexible organizing system that can span regions, organizations, and silos of all kinds, impact networks underlie some of the most impressive and large-scale efforts to create change across the globe. 
 
David Ehrlichman draws on his experience as a network builder; interviews with dozens of network leaders; and insights from the fields of network science, community building, and systems thinking to provide a clear process for creating and developing impact networks. Given the increasing complexity of our society and the issues we face, our ability to form, grow, and work through networks has never been more essential.

Name: Christine Carrillio
Description:
On paper, everything looked great.
 Built three businesses to $200M in revenue
 Bootstrapped a service business to $25M with 25 people in five years
 Scaled a VC-backed tech company to $28M ARR in under two years with just 12 people
 Made a double-sided consumer marketplace profitable in three months with a five-person team
 Raised $30M in venture capital

But behind the scenes, I was exhausted, constantly putting out fires, and convinced the business would fall apart if I stepped away.

So I pushed harder, thinking that was the only way.

Then life forced me to stop.

I ran headfirst into a glass window. A concussion took me out for eight weeks. No emails, no meetings, no problem-solving. For the first time, I couldn’t step in to fix things.

And when I came back, I was shocked. The business hadn’t just survived. It had thrived.

That moment forced me to question everything.

I always thought the key to scaling was working harder and building better systems. And systems did help. But what really made the difference wasn’t the business. It was me.

I had built a company that depended on me, not because it had to, but because I didn’t know how to let go. I believed that stepping back meant losing control. That if I wasn’t involved in everything, things would fall apart.

That belief had to change.

Learning to trust my team, step back, and stop being the bottleneck wasn’t easy. But once I did, everything changed.

The systems worked because I let them. My team thrived because I gave them space. And the business grew faster than ever, not because I was in every decision, but because I wasn’t.

Now, I help founders do the same.

Most founders come to me because they feel stuck. They’re scaling, but at the cost of their time, health, and freedom. It doesn’t have to be that way.

If you’re ready to build a business that grows without you being in the weeds, join me for daily insights on scaling, leadership, and personal growth.

Let’s get started.

Name: Dog-Eared Philosophy
Description:
I am a creative person with a passion for storytelling and spiritual connections.  My work is all about the art of communication-whether it’s through a compelling story, a thought-provoking illustration, or a deep, one-on-one conversation.  I believe that creativity and spirituality are powerful tools for personal growth, understanding and finding clarity on life’s purpose.  As a writer and illustrator, I specialize in crafting narratives that resonate on a deeper level.  I love bringing stories to life visually, creating a space for curiosity and wonder.  I’m always looking to connect with others who share a passion for creativity, self-discovery, and building a more connected world.

My journey began before I was brought into this world.  I have always believed in a Higher Power, that I call God. I can look back at many pivotal moments in my life and say, “that was God’s plan” or “that was one of God’s many miracles.”  God pulled me from the trenches of Alcoholism and provided a path forward to carry out His will, not mine.  I have spent the past many years reading books that speak to me, that help me grow spiritually, provide daily inspiration and help me understand my soul’s purpose.  So, I’ve decided to take my dog-eared pages from books I’ve read and share them with you.  I will share my thoughts, and I hope to inspire others and connect with my brothers and sisters that want to share their own thoughts and experiences.

When we go to Heaven it will not be about what we’ve accomplished in our career, finances, or prestigious awards.  We are judged on our connection with people, nature, and God Himself.

Name: 镜中InTheMirror
Description:
InTheMirror Bookstore is located at the foot of Lu Mountain in Changsha, Hunan province. It is a charming bookstore that has captured the hearts of many readers with its unique ambiance and profound cultural significance. 
The proprietor of Bookstore is named Heidi. She recalls hosting a book launch event for Shanghai writer Gu Xiang's new book "Zhaoqiao Village" under a 160-year-old ancient tree in Tea Plantation Village back in September 2019. Since then, she has nurtured the idea of opening a bookstore in this serene location. The interior of the bookstore is simple yet elegant, creating a peaceful atmosphere for reading.

InTheMirror Bookstore boasts a diverse collection of books that covers various fields and genres. Whether you're interested in literature, history, art, or science, you can find books that cater to your tastes. Additionally, the bookstore regularly organizes cultural activities, including book sharing sessions, author lectures, and art exhibitions, providing readers with more opportunities for communication and learning.

With its enchanting ambiance and rich cultural significance, Mirror Bookstore has become one of the most beautiful bookstores in Changsha. Here, you can leisurely stroll amidst a sea of books and experience the joy and inspiration that reading brings. Whether you are a local resident or a visitor, a visit to InTheMirror Bookstore is definitely worth your while.

Name: CuriousBeeBooks
Description:
Discover a world where stories empower children and embrace neurodiversity.
The books are designed for adults to read to children. They  are engaging, enjoyable and informative for all ages, and are meant to prompt discussion.
They are written with the intention of informing adults and children about neurodiversity and behaviour traits, with tips and ideas of how to help too. They answer a lot of questions which parents ask when they come to my therapy and parenting sessions with their children.
My books are written in simple enjoyable story form and explore some of the emotional and behavioural challenges children, and parents, face today.
They are an enjoyable read for all ages, exploring themes of ADHD, Autism and dyslexia and more, as well as topics such as anxiety, anger, frustration and grief. 
I have realised over the past few years in my therapy sessions that many children and parents need more information and support with children who may be on the spectrum.
In a world where waiting lists for diagnosis are so long, this inspired me to create a series of books which aim to give an initial insight into individual differences and to suggest helpful tips for families as they battle with knowing what to do and how to help.
The books also aim to inform neurotypical people to promote acceptance. I believe there is a need for neurotypical people to understand and accept the differences in all individuals, especially those who are neurodiverse.

Name: To Lose A Dog
Description:
To Lose a Dog is a memoir and self help hybrid that explores love, loss, and the journey of learning to live again after the death of a beloved companion. Through honest storytelling, emotional insight, and practical guidance, the book offers support for anyone navigating grief, especially the unique heartbreak of losing a pet.

This project was created to help people feel less alone. It blends personal narrative with lessons on presence, resilience, acceptance, and rebuilding a life with meaning. Whether someone is processing fresh grief or reflecting years later, the book provides a space to understand emotions, find comfort, and move forward with intention.

The mission of To Lose a Dog is simple:
To give readers a language for their pain, a companion through healing, and a path back to themselves.

Name: Amethyst And Ink LLc
Description:
Amethyst and Ink Books is an independent, woman-owned bookstore based in Lawton, Oklahoma, established in 2025 to meet a growing local demand for accessible, thoughtfully curated literature. As the only new-book retail bookstore in the area, Amethyst and Ink fills a vital gap in the local cultural and retail landscape by offering a broad selection of new titles for readers of all ages.

The business operates through a hybrid model that includes pop-up events, online retail, and a vision for a permanent brick-and-mortar location. Inventory includes genres such as fiction, romance, thrillers, graphic novels, children’s books, and personal development titles, as well as complementary items like journals, bookmarks, and reader-focused gifts.

Rooted in a mission to foster literacy, connection, and community, the store also plans to host group events, reading circles, and educational meetups. With strong community ties and a unique aesthetic identity, Amethyst and Ink Books aims to be more than a bookstore—it strives to become a hub for storytelling, creativity, and local engagement.

Description:
The Birth of a Nation Abraham, Isaac and Jacob by David E Files

My Name Is David E Files Sr. I Am 69 Years Old And A Minister In The Church Of God. About Two Years Ago After Years Of Pain My Back Went Completely Out And I Was Forced To Retire. I Prayed Everyday For The Lord To Heal My Back So I Could Go Back To Work. Then In October Of 2024 As I Was Praying About Not Being Able To Work And Help My Wife With The Bills. It Seemed Like The Lord Spoke To My Heart And Said "You Can Write." I Said Okay Lord What Do You Want Me To Write About? And Immediately I Knew What The Lord Wanted Me To Write About. And This Is How This Book Came To Be. All Praise Goes To God. He Is So Good To His Children.

Name: Quiero Libros SRL
Description:
Soy Kary, librera desde la cuna. Me presento para los y las que no conocen el origen de Quiero libros.
.
Mis padres fueron los fundadores de las LIBRERIAS YENNY, por lo cual nací entre libros.
.
El gordo Skidelsky (mi papá) un día decidió que las librerías tenían que estar presentes en los shoppings y como familia lo acompañamos. Siendo grandes lectores, sabíamos que no había nada más lindo que disfrutar del momento de elegir un libro y por eso le dimos a nuestros clientes la posibilidad de sentarse como en el living de su casa cuando nos visitaban
.
Años después me dieron la libertad de crear los espacios infantiles. Trencitos, casas con almohadones, anfiteatros para presentaciones y talleres para chicos y grandes. Hasta cocinamos y festejamos cumpleaños infantiles!
.
Al dejar Yenny, vino Capítulo Dos, donde seguimos cumpliendo sueños. Lanzamientos de los libros de HARRY POTTER, presentaciones, talleres y otras locuras literarias que les iré contando.
.
Trabajé para grandes y pequeñas editoriales en sus stands de las diferentes ferias de libro creando sectores infantiles, lanzamientos de libros, talleres para chicos.
.
Tuve el placer de crecer entre grandes referentes de la literatura. Pueden encontrar algunas anécdotas libreras en nuestro feed (vendrán más en este año).
.
Desde hace algunos años con Gaby, mi marido, creamos Quiero libros. Un showroom con propuestas para la familia y asesoramiento personalizado presencial y online.
.
 El año pasado, hicimos vivos, recomendamos libros para chicos y grandes y mucho más.

Notre mission : offrir aux enfants et aux adolescents une sélection soignée et inspirante de livres qui nourrissent l’imagination et l’émerveillement.

L’Entre-Mondes aspire à devenir un sanctuaire de l’imaginaire pour les jeunes lecteurs. Un lieu vivant, enchanteur et bienveillant, où chacun peut se sentir accueilli, reconnu et libre d’explorer. Nos portes s’ouvrent sur des mondes mythiques, peuplés de mystères et de magie, où chaque lecture devient une aventure.

Nous mettons de l’avant des œuvres de grande qualité, tant par leur contenu que par leur présentation, pour faire de chaque lecture une expérience sensorielle et intellectuelle enrichissante. À travers nos choix éditoriaux, nos espaces de lecture immersifs et nos activités culturelles, nous souhaitons cultiver un lien profond entre les jeunes et les univers littéraires fantastiques, tout en encourageant la découverte de soi, la pensée critique et l’ouverture aux autres.

Name: Kay Craddock Antiquarian Bookseller
Description:
At Kay Craddock - Antiquarian Bookseller, we specialise in cataloging and preserving books and other related, endeavoring to capture and present the magic of rare and old books. Our journey began in 1965 when we opened our doors as "The Essendon Treasure Chest," a family venture founded by Kay Craddock and her parents, Leslie and Muriel Craddock. Two years later we re-established the business in Melbourne's CBD. Over the past seven decades, our commitment to the world of books has matured and thrived.

We enjoy the juxtaposition of being a general secondhand as well as an antiquarian bookseller, embracing all aspects of print on paper, from the cradle of printing in the 15th century through to authors of the 21st century, many of whom will become keenly sought after by collectors of the future.

We take pride in offering a wide range of literary treasures, from rare manuscripts and historical documents to modern and nostalgic fiction. The diversity of our stock, housed in an historic setting, makes our bookshop a haven for collectors, scholars, and book enthusiasts.

Legacy of Excellence: Over half a century of experience in the secondhand/antiquarian book trade.

Historical Significance: An array of books that offer insights into history, culture, and the evolution of human thought.

Personal Service: We are committed to providing a warm and personalised experience, assisting in selecting gifts, rare acquisitions, or building private collections — and we are equally happy for you to quietly browse on your own.

Community and Engagement: We are about connecting with fellow book lovers, not only through our bookshop but also through our commitment to the annual Melbourne Rare Book Week and the Melbourne Rare Book Fair. Join our community of avid readers and collectors. Subscribe at https://www.kaycraddock.com/mailing-list.php

I am an author who writes inspirational stories to give my readers hope to take back to not only their lives but the romance in their lives. I believe that the real fairy tale begins and ends at home with simple actions we can do for one another that build love and trust. Find out how in:

"The Gift Of The Music Maker"

When love is lost, pick up the PIECES and carry them
until new love is found and life’s puzzle is solved.

"The Last Slow Dance"

To be selfless in the name of LOVE, is to be a blessing and to be blessed.
Marriage is a lifelong romance; a song to be danced slowly to, FOREVER.

I am a professional counselor in my private life and a business owner. Along with promoting my books, I also showcase authors of merit with stories I believe in. 

I am currently mentored by RICHARD PAUL EVANS, #1 NY Times bestselling author.  I was awarded the Author Ready Award for my work through his mentorship program.

I was a reporter for the Spartanburg Business Journal.
Published by Woman's World Magazine as the cover story in 2005.
Books under review for possible film representation
THE LAST SLOW DANCE and THE GIFT OF THE MUSIC MAKER are currently sold at AMAZON and BARNES AND NOBLE.

There is a story of pain and blessing behind the creation of my books. 

My personal writing journey was published in the Spartanburg Herald Journal.

My recent interview with TheAuthorShow tells my story of destiny and hope and realizing that we are each important enough to be saved just when we were ready to give up.  Listen on my website henributlerbooks.com

Name: WHY GOD SLEEPS WHEN WE WAKE UP
Description:
WHY GOD SLEEPS WHEN WE WAKE-UP

WHY GOD SLEEPS WHEN WE WAKE-UP is a groundbreaking work by award-winning author Ramzi Najjar. More than a book, it is a wake-up call for an age overwhelmed by noise, illusions, and technological entrapment.

The book challenges readers to break free from societal distractions and distorted truths, urging a return to simplicity, clarity, and alignment with universal order. At its core, it is not about adding more to our lives, but rediscovering the essence we have buried beneath layers of performance and conditioning.

Praised internationally and honored with multiple awards, WHY GOD SLEEPS WHEN WE WAKE-UP has been recognized for its depth and cultural impact. It speaks not to seekers looking for comfort, but to individuals ready to confront reality and reclaim authorship over their lives.

This page shares insights, reviews, and discussions around the book, creating a space for those interested in exploring the deeper questions of life, consciousness, and the dynamics that govern our world.

Welcome to Be The Tortoise, where we believe in the power of creativity and imagination to shape the future. Our mission is to inspire and empower children to create their own illustrated books and even publish them, fostering a lifelong love for reading and storytelling.

About Our New Book Series: Andy Magic Maker in the Making

Our latest book series, "Andy Magic Maker in the Making," is designed to captivate young readers and ignite their imagination. Through the adventures of Andy, children will discover the magic of creativity and the joy of bringing their own stories to life.

Our Ideal Reader: Suzanne Books

Meet Suzanne Books, a caring parent who values learning and creativity. Suzanne dreams of becoming a proud parent of children who love reading. She focuses on expanding her children's imagination through reading and wants to help them develop a lifelong love for books.

Our Commitment to Parents Like Suzanne

At Be The Tortoise, we understand the challenges parents face in nurturing their children's reading habits amidst digital distractions. We provide resources and support to help parents manage screen time, create enjoyable reading routines, and choose the right books.

Encouraging Young Authors

Beyond our book series, we offer workshops and tools for children to create their own illustrated books. We believe every child has a story to tell, and we're here to help them share it with the world.

Join us in our mission to nurture curiosity and imaginative thinking in young minds. Together, let's build a future where creativity knows no bounds.

Name: Les Vendredis Astronomiques
Description:
Ce livre a été entièrement pensé, écrit et publié par trois lycéens lors de leur classe de seconde. Celui-ci regroupe des fiches explicatives sur des notions scientifiques complexes qu'ils ont essayé de vulgariser à travers quatre domaines : la physique classique, la physique quantique, l'astronomie et l'astrophysique.
Plus que le contenu, qui est en lui-même léger, parfois insuffisant et inexact, ce projet a permis de développer de nombreuses compétences comme la gestion d'équipe, le respect des différentes dead-lines, l'éveil de la curiosité scientifique, le travail de la langue anglaise et française, la communication entre les différents participants, la synthèse de données de taille important, l'adaptation de concept compliqué en analogie, l'étude des conditions et règles juridiques et administratives pour la publication d'un tel ouvrage.
L'important est de voir en ce livre l'aboutissement d'un projet, à la fois sans aucune prétention et très développé, plus que ce qu'il contient..

Name: Mangoes In The Rain
Description: In 2002, fifteen-year-old Chinaemelum leaves America, to go to Nigeria for summer break. Months later, she fights for her life against malaria. Summer is over and her father refuses to give her back her passport to return to America with her mother. She is kept in Nigeria against her will, he puts her in an all-girls boarding school, where she struggles to adapt to life, in a 3rd world country. Two and a half years later, she and her siblings escape from their father's house to the American Embassy in Lagos in hopes of leaving Nigeria. She is adamant about expressing her trauma of abandonment, abuse, and her survival mechanism then and now. Out of this tragedy, she's able to overcome and be a success today. She voices the portrayals of her childhood and teen years growing up Nigerian American in America to Nigerian parents. Raised in Silver Spring, Maryland, by a Christian Pastor and wife, life is portrayed in public as a God-fearing couple, but behind closed doors Chinaemelum witnesses her father often beating her mother. He eventually begins abusing Chinaemelum, calling her a hoar and a prostitute at age 13. Chinaemelum had high hopes when she heard she was going to Nigeria for the summer. She had no idea the gravity of the horrific adventure about to take place.

You’ll find reflections, encouragement, and leadership principles designed to support middle managers who want to stay centered, effective, and authentic in their work without losing themselves in the process.

This page exists to remind leaders in the middle that:

You don’t have to have all the answers to lead well

Calm is a leadership skill

Growth happens right where you are

Inspired by the book Leadership in the Middle, this space is for leaders who value presence, perspective, and steady leadership in real-world environments.
